UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents an individual user of the MarketScout Pro application. This entity stores user-specific data and links to an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically provided by an external authentication system.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

BusinessProfile.title: BusinessProfile
BusinessProfile.description: Represents a business entity, which can be a prospect, client, or competitor, discovered or managed within the MarketScout Pro application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BusinessProfile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns or manages this business profile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N BusinessProfile)

name.description: The official name of the business.

url.description: The primary website URL of the business.

ownerName.description: The name of the business owner or a primary contact person.

email.description: The primary contact email address for the business.

phone.description: The primary contact phone number for the business.

addressCity.description: The city part of the business's physical address.

addressCountry.description: The country part of the business's physical address.

industry.description: The industry or sector the business operates in.

socialLinks.description: An array of social media profile URLs associated with the business.

leadStatus.description: The current status of this business in the lead management pipeline.

competitorBusinessProfileIds.description: References to other BusinessProfile entities identified as competitors. (Relationship: BusinessProfile N:N BusinessProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this business profile was first created or discovered.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this business profile was last updated.

SearchQuery.title: SearchQuery
SearchQuery.description: Stores the parameters used for a specific client discovery search operation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SearchQuery entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who performed this search. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SearchQuery)

name.description: A user-defined name or description for this search query.

keywords.description: An array of keywords used in the search to find businesses.

industryFilter.description: The industry filter applied to the search, if any.

locationFilter.description: The geographic location filter applied to the search, if any.

searchDate.description: Timestamp when the search query was executed.

discoveredBusinessProfileIds.description: References to BusinessProfile entities discovered as a result of this search query. (Relationship: SearchQuery 1:N BusinessProfile)

MarketingAsset.title: MarketingAsset
MarketingAsset.description: Stores generated marketing ideas, slogans, or content outlines created by the AI marketing asset generator.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MarketingAsset entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who generated this marketing asset. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MarketingAsset)

targetBusinessProfileId.description: Reference to the BusinessProfile for which this marketing asset was generated. (Relationship: BusinessProfile 1:N MarketingAsset)

assetType.description: The type of marketing asset generated (e.g., Slogan, Marketing Idea, Brochure Outline, Logo Concept, Campaign Copy).

title.description: A brief title or summary of the marketing asset.

content.description: The generated marketing content itself (e.g., the slogan, detailed idea description, or outline text).

generationParameters.description: A JSON string storing the input parameters and context used during the AI generation process.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the marketing asset was generated.

Campaign.title: Campaign
Campaign.description: Represents a marketing campaign organized by a user, tracking its details, target businesses, associated assets, and ROI metrics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Campaign entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this campaign. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Campaign)

name.description: The name of the marketing campaign.

description.description: A detailed description of the campaign's goals and strategy.

startDate.description: The planned start date of the campaign.

endDate.description: The planned end date of the campaign.

status.description: The current status of the campaign (e.g., Planning, Active, Completed, Paused, Archived).

targetBusinessProfileIds.description: References to BusinessProfile entities that are the target audience for this campaign. (Relationship: BusinessProfile N:N Campaign)

marketingAssetIds.description: References to MarketingAsset entities used within this campaign. (Relationship: MarketingAsset N:N Campaign)

estimatedCost.description: The estimated financial cost of running this campaign.

estimatedRevenue.description: The estimated revenue or outcome expected from this campaign.

actualCost.description: The actual financial cost incurred for this campaign.

actualRevenue.description: The actual revenue or outcome achieved from this campaign.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the campaign was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the campaign was last updated.

Note.title: Note
Note.description: Represents a user-created note associated with a specific business profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Note entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this note. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Note)

businessProfileId.description: Reference to the BusinessProfile to which this note is attached. (Relationship: BusinessProfile 1:N Note)

content.description: The textual content of the note.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the note was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the note was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) MUST match the Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`). Includes denormalized 'id' matching document ID.

Stores business profiles owned by a specific user. The document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches both the path wildcard and the document's `userId` field. The 'id' field matches the document ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this business profile.

params.name: businessProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the business profile.

definition.description: Stores search queries performed by a specific user. The document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence. The 'id' field matches the document ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed this search query.

params.name: searchQueryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the search query.

definition.description: Stores marketing assets generated by a specific user. The document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence. The 'id' field matches the document ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who generated this marketing asset.

params.name: marketingAssetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the marketing asset.

definition.description: Stores marketing campaigns created by a specific user. The document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence. The 'id' field matches the document ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who created this campaign.

params.name: campaignId
params.description: The unique identifier for the marketing campaign.

definition.description: Stores notes created by a specific user and associated with a business profile. The document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence. The 'id' field matches the document ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who created this note.

params.name: noteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the note.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes strong authorization independence and clear ownership, aligning perfectly with the core design principles. All user-specific data (Business Profiles, Search Queries, Marketing Assets, Campaigns, and Notes) are nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This immediately establishes a clear, path-based ownership model where the `userId` in the path segment directly corresponds to the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`).

Authorization Independence is achieved by denormalizing the `userId` field into every child document (e.g., `BusinessProfile.userId`, `SearchQuery.userId`, etc.). This means that security rules for `/users/{userId}/businessProfiles/{businessProfileId}` can simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` and `request.auth.uid == userId` (the wildcard from the path) without needing any `get()` operations to check the parent `/users/{userId}` document. This eliminates complex authorization dependencies, simplifies rule debugging, and allows for atomic batch writes/transactions without rule failures. Each entity's `id` field will also match its document ID within its respective collection.

Query as Authorization Predicate (QAPs) is fully supported. By nesting user data, a user can only list or read documents under their specific `/users/{userId}` path. For instance, to list all `BusinessProfile`s for the current user, the client-side query would target `db.collection('users').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('businessProfiles')`. Firestore security rules would then easily enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, ensuring that users can only access their own data. The explicit `userId` field in each document provides a redundant, but crucial, check for `read` and `write` operations, reinforcing data ownership and preventing unauthorized access even if a query path was somehow manipulated. This structural segregation ensures that all documents within a given subcollection (e.g., `/users/{userId}/businessProfiles`) share the same security posture, simplifying rule sets and making them highly performant and easy to debug.
